triples.subject: electric vehicle market in the United States
triples.object: more than 315,000 vehicles sold annually from 2018 to 2020
triples.sentence: The electric vehicle market in the United States has grown from a few thousand vehicles in 2010 to more than 315,000 vehicles sold annually from 2018 to 2020.

triples.subject: electric share of new vehicle sales in 2020
triples.sentence: In 2020, the electric share of new vehicle sales was approximately 2.4%, an increase from about 2% in 2019.

triples.subject: States with zero-emission vehicle (ZEV) regulations
triples.object: a combined new electric vehicle share of 5%
triples.sentence: States with zero-emission vehicle (ZEV) regulations had a combined new electric vehicle share of 5% and typically at least 13 more electric models available than states without such regulations, which had a 1.3% average electric vehicle share.

triples.subject: States with ZEV regulations
triples.object: about two-thirds of 2020 U.S. electric vehicle sales
triples.sentence: States with ZEV regulations were responsible for about two-thirds of 2020 U.S. electric vehicle sales and less than one-third of overall light-duty vehicle sales.

triples.subject: top 11 metropolitan areas with the highest uptake
triples.object: substantial consumer incentives ranging from $1,500 to more than $5,500
triples.sentence: The top 11 metropolitan areas with the highest uptake had substantial consumer incentives ranging from $1,500 to more than $5,500.

triples.subject: ten with the highest uptake
triples.sentence: Of the 200 most populous metropolitan areas, the ten with the highest uptake averaged a 10% electric share and 935 public chargers per million population.

triples.subject: U.S. population
triples.object: an area with just 20% of the public charging availability in those top-ten markets
triples.sentence: population lives in an area with just 20% of the public charging availability in those top-ten markets.
